sutta: once the exalted 1 with the great following of monks walked a tour among the coastal lands and as the exalted 1 went along the highway he saw a place where grew a big growth of salt trees and seeing it there he moved down from the road and went towards it and made its way his way among the trees and at a certain place he smiled now the venerable ananda thought what caused now what reason moved the exhorted 1 to show a smile not for nothing do tattagata smile and the venable ananda asked him concerning the matter then said the exalted 1 at this place ananda in olden times there was a rich and flourishing city thronged with many people and by the city ananda there dwelt the exalted 1 kasapa harahan fully enlightened and gave was a lay disciple of the exalted 1 kasapa but he kept not the moral precepts now because of gave sin there were about 500 who testified and were stirred to discipleship but they kept not the moral precepts thought he i have greatly served these 500 lay disciples being the first to move and disturb myself yet i keep not the moral precepts nor do these 500 others this is a leveling of levels leaving no width of a mole come now i'm for something more i stop here for a while this gave he had 500 friends and he was the first to become a disciple of the buddha kasapa which was the buddha previous to our sakyamuni buddha and when he became a disciple of the kasapa buddha his 500 friends also became disciples of kasapa buddha but all of them did not keep the precepts so he said this is a leveling of levels leaving no width of a more that means this is the lowest you know lowers and the stage there at la so he said i'm for something more he should uh aim for something higher now the suitor continues so gawaisin went up to the 500 and said no good sirs that from today i keep the moral precepts then ananda those 500 thought to themselves verily master gave sin has greatly served us being first to move and destroy himself and this same master gave sin will now keep the moral precepts why then not we too then when those 500 lay disciples took away sin and told him that they too henceforth would keep the moral precepts again thought gavaisin this also is a leveling of levels leaving no width of a for something more and he went and told them saying no sirs from today i follow the celebrates life the life remote giving up sex life common among men then taught those others why not we too and did likewise again thought ingave sin this is also a leveling of levels leaving no width of more time for something more and went and told them saying no sirs from today i am a 1 kneeler and abstain from night eating giving up eating at wrong times then thought they why not we too ended likewise now the lay disciple gave sin considered thus i have greatly served these 500 lay disciples being the first to move and disturb myself i have kept the moral precepts and now they too have done this i have followed the live remote and now they too have done this i have become a 1 kneeler and now they too have done this this is indeed a leveling of levels leaving no width of a more come now i'm for something more and ananda the lay disciple gave sin went to the exalted 1 kasapa arahan sambuda and having come said to him lord grant that i may go forth beside the exalted 1 grant me full ordination and away sin delay disciple obtain permission to go forth beside kasapa the exalted 1 arahan sama sambuddha and he obtained full ordination now ananda not long after his ordination conveys in the monk living alone withdrawn diligent ardent resolved entered into and abort in that unsurpassed goal of the holy life here in this very life realizing it by his own knowledge that goal for the good of which clansmen sons rightly go forth from the home to the homeless life and he declared this destroyed his birth lived is the holy life done is the task there is no more of this to come thereafter ananda the monk of a sin was numbered among the arahants then taught those 500 lay disciples master gaway sin has done us a great service he was the first to move and bestow himself and now he has had his hair and beard shaved off dawn the yellow rope and goes forth from the home to the homeless life why then not we too and those 500 lay disciples and nanda went and begged the exalted 1 kasapa saying grant lord that we may go forth beside the exalted 1 grant us full ordination and those 500 obtain permission then taught the monk away sin i verily can obtain this unsupport unsurpassed bliss of liberation at will easily and without difficulty would that those 500 monks could obtain this bliss in light manner ananda those 500 monks about alone withdrawn zealous ardent result and not long after entered and dwelt in that unsurpassed goal of the holy life here in this very life realizing it by their own knowledge that for which cleansement sons rightly go forth and declared destroyed is birth lived is the holy life done is the task there is no more of this to come thus verily ananda these 5 100 monks with convey sin at their head in striving from higher things to higher from strength to strength came to realize a liberation above which there is no higher wherefore ananda train yourselves in this way from higher to higher from strength to strength we will strive and will come to realize the liberation above which there is no higher verily does ananda train yourselves that's the end of the suta
commentary: this is quite an inspiring suta and it shows how good friends help each other in life pulling each other up along the way and this gave he was a very good friend to these 500 people he was the first to place the trail and show them the way he was the first to become a disciple then kept the precepts then kept the uh 8 precepts and finally renounced and became an arahant and they followed him and they also walked the good way so in this shows when we have a good friend a friend who knows the dhamma and he can inspire us and that's very good then we because we are generally quite influenced by our friends if we join with friends who are not interested in the dhamma who walk the evil way the foolish way and we will also end up back but when we join with good friends then they help us and we and we help each other along the path there is 1 in 1 sutra ananda told the buddha he said he thinks and that half of the holy life have to do with good friends and the buddha said no and the whole of the holy life has to do with good friends so good friends in the spiritual path is very important we inspire each other and help each other in the last partner you note now that the 500 manga and also gave the abode alone withdrawn zealous ardent resolved and not long after entered and dwelt in that unsurpassed goal of the holy life so you see generally and these are hands and they to obtain the highest state and they lived alone when you live alone there's nobody to distract you to talk to then you spend more time in meditation undisturbed and then the progress with me much faster sometimes if you don't stay alone we stay in company like in the monastery then it is good to practice aloofness that means we don't talk too much even though we are in the company of other people in the monastery yeah we try to limit our talk and practice more the nexus is
